summary:
In FINGERPRINTING (FINDER), both thumbs are omitted because they lack distinct ridges and have a different anatomy than other digits; focusing only on non-thumb fingers ensures clearer, more reliable print recordings suitable for algorithmic analysis. The omission simplifies processes while maintaining diagnostic utility through comprehensive yet standardized sampling across remaining eight fingers. To directly answer your query regarding exclusions within FINGERPRINTING (FINDER)—the two pairs specifically left out involve each individual thumb—resultantly totaling one set per hand being excised entirely during procedure execution phase.
